title: mentha suaveolens var. serrata
intro: Mentha suaveolens var. serrata, also known as Round-leaf Pennyroyal, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of carvone and limonene in its essential oil. The Round-leaf Pennyroyal has been traditionally used to alleviate digestive issues, such as indigestion and nausea, and its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties make it beneficial for treating menstrual cramps, headaches, and fever. Herbal preparations of Mentha suaveolens var. serrata include teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to relieve respiratory issues, improve digestion, and promote relaxation.
